name: batch-flood
title: Batch flood - 200 async items through the Redis Streams worker
narrative: Submits 200 chat requests as async batch jobs (the API caps a batch at 100 items, so this is two jobs). Each create is one transaction (job + items + outbox row) and one Redis Streams publish; the control-worker claims items with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED and processes them on its bounded pool (WORKER_CONCURRENCY=8) against the same backends. The driver polls both jobs to a terminal status and reports wall-clock throughput.
watch: Grafana: 'Batch processing' and the 'Batch items / s' stat; the worker is a separate process - this traffic never touches the gateway's chat handler.
